title: QUIC CRT server client-auth transcript/runtime prerequisite work item
completion_notes: This work item intentionally stops short of public listener acceptance. The validator may continue to reject ClientCertificateRequired until the internal transcript/runtime prerequisite is proven and the public boundary is revisited separately.
out_of_scope: broader client-auth policy beyond the requested server-auth slice
summary: Add the smallest internal managed server-auth prerequisite needed to emit CertificateRequest and accept an inbound client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished sequence through the existing transcript and bridge seams without widening the public server surface yet.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for the internal server-auth path, re-run the current CRT and API guards that must remain unchanged, re-run the full REQ_QUIC_CRT_ sweep, and run repo-local SpecTrace validation/render checks after the canonical JSON updates.
planned_changes:
- Thread a server-client-auth-required flag and callback through the existing runtime/bridge seam.
- Emit one TLS 1.3 CertificateRequest on the server flight when the internal prerequisite flag is enabled.
- Accept an inbound client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished sequence in the server-role transcript and key schedule.
- Surface client-certificate acceptance only through the existing callback seam and fail closed if acceptance is not granted.
- Keep the public listener validator rejection for ClientCertificateRequired in place until the full public boundary is revisited.
- Add positive and negative requirement-home tests that prove the internal prerequisite slice without claiming broader PKI, 0-RTT, transfer, retry, or key-update behavior.
